name: leek pepper lentil skillet
instructions: Sauté leeks, bell peppers, and garlic in olive oil over medium heat. Season with salt, black pepper, paprika, and thyme. Once the vegetables are softened, mix in raw lentils. Cook them dry — add just enough water to keep things from sticking as the lentils tenderize, letting the moisture cook off rather than building a stew. Adjust salt at the end.
notes:
A clean mediterranean lentil dish that needs neither stock nor tomatoes — but it isn't a stew. The skillet format keeps it dry; the leek + thyme + paprika combination carries the flavor without any of the usual lentil-stew supports.

LEEK + THYME: this dish is one of the best uses of the leek-thyme combination outside of soup contexts. Along with mexican cooking, leek-and-thyme mediterranean skillets are where thyme really earns its keep — paired here with bell peppers and paprika rather than the cumin/oregano of the mexican axis.

NO STOCK, NO TOMATOES: deliberate. Stock would push this toward a chicken-and-vegetable stew; tomatoes would push it italian. Holding both out keeps the dish in its own distinct mediterranean territory.

CREAM AND WHITE WINE: optional secondary additions for a richer version. White wine to deglaze, heavy cream to finish. Cream gives the fully french version; without it the dish stays cleaner and more vegetable-forward (closer to the mediterranean end of french cooking).
